content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
February 22,
1995
John G.
Barisone
City Attorney
City of Santa Cruz
333 Church Street
Santa Cruz, California
95060
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A—95—O11
Dear Mr.
Barisone:
You have requested advice on behalf of Santa Cruz City
Councilmember Celia Scott regarding the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the “Act.”)
1
OUESTION
May Councilmember Scott participate in decisions regarding
transactions between the City of Santa Cruz and the Citizens
Committee for the Homeless
(“CCH”),
since she serves as
a
non—
salaried member of the Board of Directors of
CCH?
CONCLUSION
As
a non—salaried member of the Board of Directors of
CCH,
Councilineinber Scott does not have a financial interest in
CCH.
Therefore,
Councilmember Scott does not have a conflict of
interest under the Act.
However,
other provisions may apply; please refer to
Councilmeinber Scott’s statement of incompatible activities to
ascertain if there is a problem with her being on the
CCH Board
relative to her city council position.
FACTS
Councilinember Scott serves as a non—salaried member of the
Citizens Committee for the Homeless
(“CCH”).
You have also stated
Government Code Sections 81000—91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations,
Sections
18000-18954.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California
Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800
•
P.O. Box 807
•
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807
•
(916) 322-5660


File No.
A—95--11
Page
2
that Ms. Scott does not receive any other income from CCH,
such as
reimbursement for travel.
The mission of
CCH is to address
various issues concerning the homeless population in Santa Cruz
County,
such as shelter needs, health care needs,
employment needs
and substance abuse.
In many instances,- CCH works closely with
the City of Santa Cruz in an effort to effectively and efficiently
address social problems concerning the city’s homeless population.
As a result, it from time to time applies to the city for grant
funds to finance its various programs, enters into contracts with
the city to provide certain services to the city’s homeless
population and seeks land use approvals to facilitate
implementation of its various programs within the city.
ANALYS IS
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making,
participating in, or using his official position to influence a
governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to know he
has a financial interest.
An official has a financial interest in
a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will
have a material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect
on the public generally,
on the official or a member of his
immediate family, or on:
(a)
Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment worth
one thousand dollars
($1,000)
or more.
-
(b)
Any real property in which the public
official has a direct or indirect interest worth
one thousand dollars
($1,000)
or more.
(c)
Any source of income,
other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official status,
aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars
($250)
or more in value provided to,
received by or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.
(d)
Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee,
or holds any position of management.
(e)
Any donor of,
or any intermediary or
agent for a donor of,
a gift or gifts aggregating
two hundred fifty dollars
($250)
or more in value
provided to,
received by,
or promised to the public


File No.
A—95—11
Page
3
official within 12 months prior to the time when
the decision is made.
Section 87103.
As a public official
(Section 82048),
Councilmember Scott
may not make, participate in making, or in any way attempt to
influence a decision in which she has a financial interest.
(Section 87100.)
Since CCH is a nonprofit organization, it is not a “business
entity” as defined in Section 82005.
Thus,
CCH is not a
disqualifying financial interest for purposes of Section 87103 (d).
(Eymil Advice Letter,
No.
1-93-177.)
Furthermore,
CCH is not a
source of income to Ms. Scott since she serves as a non—salaried
member of the Board of Directors.
Therefore,
under the Act,
she
does not have a financial interest which may be affected by any
decisions regarding transactions between the City of Santa Cruz
and CCH.
Our advice to you is limited to the provisions of the
Political Reform Act.
Please review the statement of incompatible
activities for city councilmembers to determine whether other
provisions apply to Ms. Scott’s situation.
I trust this answers your question.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
JiJJl
S echer
Counsel,
Legal Division
SGC:JS:ak
